metadata.security_proposition: The rapid multi-username NTLM failure burst is adversary-driven credential spraying.

attack_category.category: Brute Force (password spray / username enumeration — failed, no compromise)
attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = WEAK. A burst of 50 failed NTLM logons (EventID 4625) in ~1 second, across 50 DISTINCT non-existent usernames, every one returning SubStatus 0xc0000064 (user does not exist), on win-host-447. This is a genuine automated brute-force / spray INDICATOR — but the evidence is INSUFFICIENT to confirm any compromise or impact: there is NO 4624 success anywhere, and not one valid account was even found (all attempts hit non-existent users). Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): a model must NOT escalate to 'confirmed credential-access breach / High' on the raw failure count; the correct read is a low-impact failed spray attempt. NOTE: PurpleSharp-simulated (hence LogonType 2 / local source) — the evidence PATTERN (failed auth burst, no success) is what the case tests, not the simulation artifacts. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: All 50 failed-logon (4625) records are on the single host win-host-447; single sensor (Security auth). The case is scoped to that host's 4625 records; any other host/event in the file is excluded.

boundary_definition: A0 = the first failed NTLM logon (4625) of the burst (335396, 19:03:49).
EV = the remaining 49 failed NTLM logons (each a different non-existent username), 335397-335445, all within ~1 second.
Scoped to win-host-447's 4625 records (all 50). The paired 4776 (NTLM credential-validation) records and any other-host events are excluded to keep the case to the failed-logon evidence.
ABSENT by design: any 4624 success (checked, none) — the outcome that would confirm compromise is not present; and no valid account was hit (all 0xc0000064).

main_alert_selection.selection_method: A burst of failed logons (EventID 4625) is the canonical brute-force / password-spray detection trigger.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: A0 is the first failed NTLM logon; the following 49 (distinct non-existent users, ~1 second) establish the automated-spray pattern.

event_groups.description: 50 failed NTLM logons in ~1 second, 50 distinct non-existent usernames, all SubStatus 0xc0000064, no success.

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: WEAK / over-triage-prone. A failed-logon burst is a real spray indicator, but with no success and no valid account hit, the evidence cannot confirm compromise or meaningful impact. The model must not over-escalate.
The decisive facts: (1) zero 4624 successes; (2) all 50 attempts return 0xc0000064 (user does not exist) — the attacker's userlist was entirely wrong. Low impact; insufficient to confirm compromise.

PurpleSharp-simulated, so LogonType=2 and a local/self source (IpAddress='-') rather than a remote type-3 spray. This is a data-provenance caveat; the evidence-sufficiency judgment (failed burst, no compromise) is unaffected.

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
